questions.stem: This diagram shows the segmented body plan of a helminth whose individual segments each contain reproductive organs. What are these segments called?

explanation.summary: Cestodes (tapeworms) have a scolex (head) and a chain of segments called proglottids; each mature proglottid is hermaphroditic, containing both male and female organs.
explanation.whyCorrect: Cestodes (tapeworms) have a scolex (head) and a chain of segments called proglottids; each mature proglottid is hermaphroditic, containing both male and female organs.

questions.stem: This electron micrograph shows a slender female worm held within a groove of the thicker male. What is this male body groove called?

explanation.summary: Adult schistosomes (blood flukes) are unusual among trematodes in having separate sexes; the male's gynecophoral canal holds the female during continuous mating.
explanation.whyCorrect: Adult schistosomes (blood flukes) are unusual among trematodes in having separate sexes; the male's gynecophoral canal holds the female during continuous mating.

questions.stem: A skin scraping viewed under the microscope reveals this organism along with surrounding eggs. What is the diagnostic finding?

explanation.summary: Definitive diagnosis of scabies is made by scraping a burrow and identifying the Sarcoptes scabiei mite, its eggs, or fecal pellets microscopically.
explanation.whyCorrect: Definitive diagnosis of scabies is made by scraping a burrow and identifying the Sarcoptes scabiei mite, its eggs, or fecal pellets microscopically.

questions.stem: This stool-smear organism has two nuclei and multiple flagella, giving it a face-like appearance. How does it move?

explanation.summary: Giardia lamblia is a flagellated protozoan; its paired nuclei and flagella produce the classic 'owl-face' trophozoite that causes fatty, foul-smelling diarrhea.
explanation.whyCorrect: Giardia lamblia is a flagellated protozoan; its paired nuclei and flagella produce the classic 'owl-face' trophozoite that causes fatty, foul-smelling diarrhea.

questions.stem: This large oval protozoan has a fuzzy border made of thousands of tiny structures. What is its mode of locomotion?

explanation.summary: Balantidium coli is the only ciliate known to infect humans; the fuzzy edge is thousands of cilia. It is transmitted fecal-orally and can cause dysentery.
explanation.whyCorrect: Balantidium coli is the only ciliate known to infect humans; the fuzzy edge is thousands of cilia. It is transmitted fecal-orally and can cause dysentery.

questions.stem: These intensely itchy skin findings are worst at night and cluster in finger webs and skin folds. The tunnel-like burrow lines seen are best described as:

explanation.summary: Scabies mites tunnel through the epidermis, producing serpiginous burrow lines; lesions favor finger webs, wrists, waistline, and genitals and itch worst at night.
explanation.whyCorrect: Scabies mites tunnel through the epidermis, producing serpiginous burrow lines; lesions favor finger webs, wrists, waistline, and genitals and itch worst at night.

questions.stem: The arthropod shown is considered the deadliest animal on earth to humans. Why?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It burrows into skin and causes an intense hypersensitivity reaction

choices.label: B
choices.text: It transmits disease only mechanically on its body surface

choices.label: C
choices.text: It multiplies within human tissue as an endoparasite

choices.label: D
choices.text: Its bite injects a directly lethal neurotoxin

choices.label: E
choices.text: It serves as a biological vector transmitting parasites, bacteria, and viruses

explanation.summary: Mosquitoes are the deadliest animal to humans because they act as biological vectors, spreading malaria, arboviruses, filariasis, and other pathogens that kill millions annually.
explanation.whyCorrect: Mosquitoes are the deadliest animal to humans because they act as biological vectors, spreading malaria, arboviruses, filariasis, and other pathogens that kill millions annually.

questions.stem: This schematic depicts a mite burrowing within the layers of host skin. What type of parasite is responsible?

explanation.summary: Scabies is caused by Sarcoptes scabiei, an ectoparasitic mite that only superficially burrows into the epidermis rather than invading deeper tissues; the itch is a Type I hypersensitivity reaction.
explanation.whyCorrect: Scabies is caused by Sarcoptes scabiei, an ectoparasitic mite that only superficially burrows into the epidermis rather than invading deeper tissues; the itch is a Type I hypersensitivity reaction.
